summary: Internal status page dashboard for a district IT team. No login required for viewers. Techs can log in to the admin panel to post and update incidents and resolutions. Simple, clean UI visible across the district.
architectural_notes: Public read-only view; no authentication needed for viewers
Admin panel now requires login authentication; techs must log in to create/update incidents
known_issues: Dashboard incorrectly shows 'no incidents' or stale state even after incidents are added via the admin panel
Frontend fails to connect to the backend, showing 'Failed to load incidents / Unable to connect to the backend' error on the dashboard
Light/dark mode toggle implementation broke the backend connection, causing 'Failed to load incidents / Unable to connect to the backend' error

existing_features.title: Build a public-facing status dashboard page that displays all active and resolved incidents grouped by status. Show each incident's title, affected system, severity, current status, last updated time, and its update timeline. Active incidents should appear prominently at the top. No login is required to view this page.

Build a technician admin panel accessible via a distinct route (e.g., /admin) that allows techs to create new incidents, add update messages to existing incidents, and change the status of incidents. No authentication gate is required, but the admin panel should be clearly separated from the public view.

Apply a clean, professional visual theme suited for an internal IT operations tool. Use a dark or neutral system-monitor aesthetic with clear status color coding: red for critical, orange for major, yellow for minor, and green for resolved/informational. Typography should be sharp and legible. Layout should prioritize at-a-glance readability with minimal clutter.

Gate the /admin route behind Internet Identity authentication. Users who are not logged in must see a login prompt instead of the admin panel content. Once authenticated via Internet Identity, the full admin panel is accessible. Provide a visible login button and a logout option once authenticated.

Fix the public-facing Dashboard so that it correctly reflects the current state of incidents after they are created or updated via the admin panel. The dashboard must not show a 'no incidents' or stale state when incidents exist in the backend. Ensure the React Query cache for getAllIncidents is properly invalidated or refetched after mutations, and that the dashboard always displays the latest data on load and after polling.

Add a search/filter input to the public-facing Dashboard that allows users to filter displayed incidents by keyword. The filter should match against incident title, affected system/service, and description fields. Both active and resolved incident sections should be filtered simultaneously. The filter input should be visible above the incident list and update results in real time as the user types. If no incidents match the search term, display a clear 'no results' message.

Add a delete button to each resolved incident in the Admin Panel. Clicking the button should call the deleteIncident backend function for that incident. After a successful deletion, the React Query cache for getAllIncidents must be invalidated so the admin panel and dashboard reflect the removal immediately. The delete action should require a confirmation step (e.g., a confirmation dialog or prompt) to prevent accidental deletions. The delete button should only appear for incidents with resolved status.

Add a light/dark mode toggle to the UI that allows users to switch between a light theme and the existing dark theme. The toggle should be persistently visible in the sticky header (in the Layout component), remember the user's preference (e.g., via localStorage), and default to the system's preferred color scheme. The existing CSS custom properties in frontend/src/index.css already define both light and dark OKLCH color tokens — wire them up so toggling applies the correct token set across the entire app. The toggle should use a clear icon or labeled control (e.g., sun/moon icons) and be usable on both the Dashboard and Admin Panel.

Fix the frontend-to-backend connection so that the Dashboard and Admin Panel can successfully reach the Motoko actor. The error 'Failed to load incidents / Unable to connect to the backend' must no longer appear. Ensure the actor is correctly initialized using the useActor hook (supporting anonymous identity for public queries), that the canister ID environment variable is properly referenced, and that the useQueries hooks pass the actor instance correctly to all backend calls including getAllIncidents.

Restore the frontend-to-backend connection that was broken when the light/dark mode toggle was added. The Dashboard and Admin Panel must successfully reach the Motoko actor without showing 'Failed to load incidents / Unable to connect to the backend'. The useActor hook must be correctly initialized supporting anonymous identity for public queries, the canister ID environment variable must be properly referenced, and all useQueries hooks must pass the actor instance correctly to getAllIncidents and all other backend calls. The light/dark mode toggle (ThemeToggle, useTheme hook) must coexist with the working actor/query setup without interfering with actor initialization or query execution.

new_feature_requests.summary: Display active and resolved incidents on the status dashboard
Allow techs to create, update, and resolve incidents
No login required for read-only viewers; accessible internally across the district

Create a backend data model and actor to store incidents. Each incident must have: a unique ID, title, description, affected service/system, severity level (critical, major, minor, informational), status (investigating, identified, monitoring, resolved), timestamps for creation and last update, and an array of update entries (each with a message and timestamp). Provide CRUD operations: create incident, add update to incident, change incident status, and list all incidents.
Add login authentication to the admin panel so only authorized techs can access and update incidents
Add search/filter functionality to the dashboard to filter incidents by keyword
Allow admins to delete resolved incidents from the admin panel

Add a deleteIncident backend function to the Motoko actor that accepts an incident ID and removes the incident from the store. Only incidents with a 'resolved' status should be deletable; attempts to delete non-resolved incidents should return an error variant.
Add a light/dark mode toggle to the UI so users can switch between light and dark themes

Diagnose and fix the frontend-to-backend connection so that incidents load successfully on both the Dashboard and Admin Panel. The 'Failed to load incidents / Unable to connect to the backend' error must not appear. Audit the full data path: verify the canister ID environment variable is correctly referenced and available at runtime, confirm the useActor hook produces a valid actor instance with anonymous identity for public queries, confirm the useQueries hooks (especially getAllIncidents) receive a non-null actor and invoke backend calls correctly, and ensure no module initialization order or provider wrapping issue (e.g., from ThemeProvider, QueryClientProvider, or InternetIdentityProvider ordering in App.tsx or main.tsx) is silently preventing actor creation. Fix all identified root causes so incidents are fetched and displayed correctly on page load.
